title: Resist felting a bowl
subtitle: A firm, open vessel in Corriedale using an inflated balloon as the resist
excerpt: Wet felt a bowl 15-20 cm in diameter and 8-10 cm deep by wrapping Corriedale roving around an inflated balloon resist. When the balloon is removed and the felt fulled further, the bowl holds its shape and stands without support. Corriedale gives a firmer, harder-wearing vessel than merino.
sourceNotes: Balloon-resist felted bowls are a standard entry-level project in contemporary wet-felting instruction. Method follows the resist-wrap technique widely used in guild and workshop contexts.

content.text: Inflate a round balloon to roughly the size of a grapefruit (about 15 cm diameter). This becomes the resist that shapes the bowl interior. Corriedale is firmer and harder-wearing than merino for vessel work; a merino bowl of the same thickness is softer and more flexible.
creates the bowl mouth. The mouth diameter can be widened by making the opening cut larger; a wider cut produces a shallower bowl, a narrower cut a deeper one.

items.name: Round balloon inflated to grapefruit size

content.text: Pull roving into strips 3-4 cm wide. Wrap the balloon from the top downward, covering it in two perpendicular layers, leaving the knotted end (this will be the bowl base later) and about a 10 cm circle at the top uncovered. This uncovered area will become the bowl mouth. The overhang at the mouth edge will be folded inward.

Place the wrapped balloon on the bamboo mat. Cover with the felting net and pour hot soapy water over it. Press through the net until saturated. Rub in circular strokes through the net for 10 minutes, turning the balloon so all surfaces are worked equally. The roving should firm noticeably.
Step 3: Remove the balloon and full the bowl

Once the felt holds without the net, let the air out of the balloon through the knot or pierce it. Pull the balloon out through the mouth opening. Fold the raw mouth edge inward by about 1 cm and rub to felt it closed. Place the bowl on the mat and continue rubbing the outside with soapy palms, rotating to full all surfaces evenly. The bowl will shrink and stand more firmly as fulling progresses.
Rinse in warm then cold water. Press out moisture and place the bowl upside down over a similarly sized bowl or plate as a shaping mould while still damp. Allow to dry completely in this position.

items.fix: Re-fill with air (a new balloon of the same size works well) and continue rubbing. The felt should be clearly firm and passing the pinch test all over before the balloon is removed.
items.cause: Not felted sufficiently before the balloon came out
items.problem: The bowl collapses when the balloon is removed

items.fix: Before wetting out, add a small extra layer of roving over the knotted base area to ensure even coverage.
items.cause: Roving was wrapped thinly over the knotted end
items.problem: The base of the bowl has a circular indentation from the balloon knot
